title: Third-party driver loading during OS installation

learning.summary: Operating system installers may require third-party drivers before they can detect certain hardware. This commonly occurs with storage controllers or network adapters that are not supported by the installer.
learning.explanation:
Most operating system installers include many built-in drivers, but some hardware requires additional manufacturer-provided drivers. If the installer cannot communicate with a storage controller or network adapter, installation may not be able to continue until the appropriate driver is loaded.

During installation, the setup program may prompt for a driver when no compatible storage device or required network hardware is detected. The driver is typically supplied from removable media or another accessible location before installation proceeds.

facts.text: An installer may require third-party drivers to recognize unsupported hardware.
Storage controller drivers are a common requirement during OS installation.
Network adapter drivers may also be required during installation.

examples.text: A Windows installer cannot detect an NVMe RAID array until the vendor's storage driver is loaded from a USB drive.

examTips: If the installer cannot detect storage or networking hardware, consider loading the appropriate driver.
commonMistakes: Assuming missing hardware always indicates failed hardware instead of a missing driver.
scenarios: Load a storage controller driver when the installer reports no disks found.
pbqIdeas: Identify when third-party drivers are required during installation.

reviewNotes: Storage controller and network adapter examples were modest curriculum enrichment beyond the explicit transcript evidence.
